India raises jet fuel, commercial LPG prices as Mideast crisis drives global surge
data.description:
NEW DELHI, April 1 (Reuters) - Indian fuel retailers raised jet fuel and commercial liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) prices on Wednesday, following a sharp spike in global prices due to the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

India, the world's second-largest LPG importer, is battling its worst gas crisis in decades, with the government cutting supplies for industries to shield households from cooking gas shortages.

Domestic fuel retailers have raised prices of aviation turbine fuel by 8.6% to 104,927 rupees per kiloliter and commercial LPG by 10.4% to 2,078.50 rupees per 19-kilogram cylinder in New Delhi, the Indian Oil Corporation's website showed.

In order to insulate domestic travel costs from the substantial increase in international prices, state-owned oil marketing companies, in consultation with the Ministry of Civil Aviation, have passed only a "partial and staggered increase" to airlines, the ministry said in a post on social media.

The price increase in commercial cylinders is due to a 44% surge in the Middle Eastern benchmark Saudi Contract Price, as 20% to 30% of global LPG supplies are stuck in Strait of Hormuz, the ministry said.

The country consumed 33.15 million metric tons of LPG, or cooking gas, last year, with imports accounting for about 60% of the total. About 90% of those imports came from the Middle East.

The consumption of commercial cylinders, used by industries and hotels, is less than 10% of the total LPG consumed in the country and the prices are revised on a monthly basis, the ministry said.

The 14.2-kg domestic gas cylinder prices have been kept unchanged to protect domestic customers from the price surge, the ministry added.

To tackle the LPG crisis, India has increased domestic daily LPG production by 40% to 50,000 metric tons against a requirement of 80,000 tons and Indian companies have secured 800,000 tons of LPG cargoes from the U.S., Russia, Australia and other countries.

(Reporting by Sethuraman NR in New Delhi, Komal Salecha in Bengaluru; Editing by Sonia Cheema and Thomas Derpinghaus)

India's gross goods and services tax collections rise to 2 trillion rupees
data.description:
NEW DELHI, April 1 (Reuters) - India collected 2 trillion rupees ($21.33 billion) as goods and services tax in March, a rise of 8.8% on the year-ago period, the government said in a statement on Wednesday.

($1 = 93.7490 Indian rupees)

(Reporting by Nikunj Ohri, writing by Tanvi Mehta; Editing by YP Rajesh)

UniCredit and the Commerz Challenge: "We will be a European bank"
data.description:
(Alliance News) - UniCredit has approved its 2025 financial statements, reporting a profit of EUR8.1 billion, a EUR4.75 billion buyback, and its remuneration policy, as it prepares to ramp up its campaign in Germany.

"Alongside our organic strategy, the offer for Commerzbank reflects a broader ambition: Europe needs stronger and better-capitalized institutions," said Chairman Pier Carlo Padoan, reiterating the commitment to maintaining a constructive dialogue to foster European integration.

As reported by la Repubblica on Wednesday, the latest move by CEO Andrea Orcel was an exchange offer (OPS) for 100% of the capital, building on an existing 30% stake, at a conservative price intended to initiate negotiations. There is now a window until May to reach an agreement.

The stock closed up 1.7% yesterday, though it remains down 13% for March, in line with a banking sector hit by geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices. However, success in Germany will depend less on market forces than on the European regulatory framework, as evidenced by the failure of the 2025 bid for Banco BPM following the Italian government's use of "golden power" - a move now being contested by the EU.

"Geopolitical tensions make greater European integration, including at the regulatory level, an urgent necessity," Padoan added.

Shareholders also approved the 2025 compensation report (64.5%), the 2026 remuneration policy (93.4%), and the incentive system (96.4%). The shareholder register highlights the group's status as a public company: only BlackRock (7.6%) and Capital Research (5.3%) hold stakes above 3%, while foundations account for approximately 4%.

By Giuseppe Fabio Ciccomascolo, Alliance News senior reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com
